summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Redis-based slot locking system for high-concurrency booking protection. Backend APIs are 93.3% functional with 14/15 endpoints working correctly. Frontend slot locking integration works perfectly with proper lock acquisition, visual feedback, and dialog interactions.

flaky_endpoints.symptoms: Returns empty array instead of user's active locks
flaky_endpoints.workaround: Core locking functionality works; this is a utility endpoint issue

passed_tests: POST /api/slots/lock - Acquire soft lock (10 min TTL, SETNX atomicity)
POST /api/slots/lock with same user - Successfully refreshes existing lock
POST /api/slots/lock with different user - Returns 409 'on hold' correctly
POST /api/slots/unlock - Release lock (only owner can release), 403 for unauthorized
POST /api/slots/extend-lock - Extends to 30 min hard lock for payment processing
GET /api/venues/{id}/slots - Returns 'locked_by_you' for own locks, 'on_hold' for others
POST /api/bookings - Rejects slots locked by another user (409)
POST /api/bookings - Succeeds if locked by same user, releases lock after booking
POST /api/bookings/{id}/cancel - Successfully releases Redis lock on cancellation
Frontend: User authentication and navigation to venue detail page
Frontend: VenueDetail page shows slot legend (Available, Your Lock, On Hold, Booked)
Frontend: Clicking available slot acquires lock and opens booking dialog
Frontend: Lock status banner displayed in booking dialog ('Slot Locked - Reserved for you')
Frontend: Payment mode selection (Full Payment, Split Payment) working
Frontend: Dialog close releases lock (ESC key functionality)
Frontend: Visual slot states - calendar, time slots, pricing displayed correctly
Frontend: Toast notification 'Slot locked for 10 min' appears on lock acquisition
action_item_for_main_agent: Fix GET /api/slots/my-locks endpoint - the Redis scan_iter function is not returning active locks properly. This is a minor utility endpoint issue and does not affect core locking functionality.
